topic: Alsace sunshine, dry conditions, and grape sugar ripening
trigger_keywords: clima de Alsacia contribuye a la concentración de azúcares

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Alsace receives substantial sunshine and relatively low rainfall because the Vosges create a rain-shadow effect.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: When vines retain adequate water, sunny, comparatively dry conditions support photosynthesis and a long, healthy ripening period while limiting rain-driven berry dilution.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Grapes can reach high sugar ripeness and flavour concentration; severe water stress would instead slow photosynthesis and ripening.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Alsace's sunny, relatively dry climate can favour sugar ripening through sustained photosynthesis and limited dilution, provided water stress is not severe.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
